NKJV.1: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To the pilgrims of the Dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,
NKJV.2: elect according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, in sanctification of the Spirit, for obedience and sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ:  Grace to you and peace be multiplied.
NKJV.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who according to His abundant mercy has begotten us again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
NKJV.4: to an inheritance incorruptible and undefiled and that does not fade away, reserved in heaven for you,
NKJV.5: who are kept by the power of God through faith for salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.
NKJV.6: In this you greatly rejoice, though now for a little while, if need be, you have been grieved by various trials,
NKJV.7: that the genuineness of your faith, being much more precious than gold that perishes, though it is tested by fire, may be found to praise, honor, and glory at the revelation of Jesus Christ,
NKJV.8: whom having not seen you love. Though now you do not see Him, yet believing, you rejoice with joy inexpressible and full of glory,
NKJV.9: receiving the end of your faith—the salvation of your souls.
NKJV.10: Of this salvation the prophets have inquired and searched carefully, who prophesied of the grace that would come to you,
NKJV.11: searching what, or what manner of time, the Spirit of Christ who was in them was indicating when He testified beforehand the sufferings of Christ and the glories that would follow.
NKJV.12: To them it was revealed that, not to themselves, but to us they were ministering the things which now have been reported to you through those who have preached the gospel to you by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven—things which angels desire to look into.
NKJV.13: Therefore gird up the loins of your mind, be sober, and rest your hope fully upon the grace that is to be brought to you at the revelation of Jesus Christ;
NKJV.14: as obedient children, not conforming yourselves to the former lusts, as in your ignorance;
NKJV.15: but as He who called you is holy, you also be holy in all your conduct,
NKJV.16: because it is written, “Be holy, for I am holy.”
NKJV.17: And if you call on the Father, who without partiality judges according to each one’s work, conduct yourselves throughout the time of your stay here in fear;
NKJV.18: knowing that you were not redeemed with corruptible things, like silver or gold, from your aimless conduct received by tradition from your fathers,
NKJV.19: but with the precious blood of Christ, as of a lamb without blemish and without spot.
NKJV.20: He indeed was foreordained before the foundation of the world, but was manifest in these last times for you
NKJV.21: who through Him believe in God, who raised Him from the dead and gave Him glory, so that your faith and hope are in God.
NKJV.22: Since you have purified your souls in obeying the truth through the Spirit in sincere love of the brethren, love one another fervently with a pure heart,
NKJV.23: having been born again, not of corruptible seed but incorruptible, through the word of God which lives and abides forever,
NKJV.24: because “All flesh is as grass, And all the glory of man as the flower of the grass. The grass withers, And its flower falls away,
NKJV.25: But the word of the LORD endures forever.”   Now this is the word which by the gospel was preached to you.

ESV.1: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To those who are elect exiles of the Dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,
ESV.2: according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, in the sanctification of the Spirit, for obedience to Jesus Christ and for sprinkling with his blood: May grace and peace be multiplied to you.
ESV.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! According to his great mercy, he has caused us to be born again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
ESV.4: to an inheritance that is imperishable, undefiled, and unfading, kept in heaven for you,
ESV.5: who by God's power are being guarded through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.
ESV.6: In this you rejoice, though now for a little while, if necessary, you have been grieved by various trials,
ESV.7: so that the tested genuineness of your faith — more precious than gold that perishes though it is tested by fire — may be found to result in praise and glory and honor at the revelation of Jesus Christ.
ESV.8: Though you have not seen him, you love him. Though you do not now see him, you believe in him and rejoice with joy that is inexpressible and filled with glory,
ESV.9: obtaining the outcome of your faith, the salvation of your souls.
ESV.10: Concerning this salvation, the prophets who prophesied about the grace that was to be yours searched and inquired carefully,
ESV.11: inquiring what person or time  the Spirit of Christ in them was indicating when he predicted the sufferings of Christ and the subsequent glories.
ESV.12: It was revealed to them that they were serving not themselves but you, in the things that have now been announced to you through those who preached the good news to you by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven, things into which angels long to look.
ESV.13: Therefore, preparing your minds for action,  and being sober-minded, set your hope fully on the grace that will be brought to you at the revelation of Jesus Christ.
ESV.14: As obedient children, do not be conformed to the passions of your former ignorance,
ESV.15: but as he who called you is holy, you also be holy in all your conduct,
ESV.16: since it is written, “You shall be holy, for I am holy.”
ESV.17: And if you call on him as Father who judges impartially according to each one's deeds, conduct yourselves with fear throughout the time of your exile,
ESV.18: knowing that you were ransomed from the futile ways inherited from your forefathers, not with perishable things such as silver or gold,
ESV.19: but with the precious blood of Christ, like that of a lamb without blemish or spot.
ESV.20: He was foreknown before the foundation of the world but was made manifest in the last times for the sake of you
ESV.21: who through him are believers in God, who raised him from the dead and gave him glory, so that your faith and hope are in God.
ESV.22: Having purified your souls by your obedience to the truth for a sincere brotherly love, love one another earnestly from a pure heart,
ESV.23: since you have been born again, not of perishable seed but of imperishable, through the living and abiding word of God;
ESV.24: for “All flesh is like grass and all its glory like the flower of grass. The grass withers, and the flower falls,
ESV.25: but the word of the Lord remains forever.”  And this word is the good news that was preached to you.

NASB.1: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To those who reside as aliens, scattered throughout Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia, who are chosen
NASB.2: according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, by the sanctifying work of the Spirit, to obey Jesus Christ and be sprinkled with His blood: May grace and peace be yours in the fullest measure.
NASB.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who according to His great mercy has caused us to be born again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
NASB.4: to [obtain] an inheritance [which is] imperishable and undefiled and will not fade away, reserved in heaven for you,
NASB.5: who are protected by the power of God through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.
NASB.6: In this you greatly rejoice, even though now for a little while, if necessary, you have been distressed by various trials,
NASB.7: so that the proof of your faith, [being] more precious than gold which is perishable, even though tested by fire, may be found to result in praise and glory and honor at the revelation of Jesus Christ;
NASB.8: and though you have not seen Him, you love Him, and though you do not see Him now, but believe in Him, you greatly rejoice with joy inexpressible and full of glory,
NASB.9: obtaining as the outcome of your faith the salvation of your souls.
NASB.10: As to this salvation, the prophets who prophesied of the grace that [would come] to you made careful searches and inquiries,
NASB.11: seeking to know what person or time the Spirit of Christ within them was indicating as He predicted the sufferings of Christ and the glories to follow.
NASB.12: It was revealed to them that they were not serving themselves, but you, in these things which now have been announced to you through those who preached the gospel to you by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven-- things into which angels long to look.
NASB.13: Therefore, prepare your minds for action, keep sober [in spirit], fix your hope completely on the grace to be brought to you at the revelation of Jesus Christ.
NASB.14: As obedient children, do not be conformed to the former lusts [which were yours] in your ignorance,
NASB.15: but like the Holy One who called you, be holy yourselves also in all [your] behavior;
NASB.16: because it is written, 'YOU SHALL BE HOLY, FOR I AM HOLY.'
NASB.17: If you address as Father the One who impartially judges according to each one's work, conduct yourselves in fear during the time of your stay [on earth];
NASB.18: knowing that you were not redeemed with perishable things like silver or gold from your futile way of life inherited from your forefathers,
NASB.19: but with precious blood, as of a lamb unblemished and spotless, [the blood] of Christ.
NASB.20: For He was foreknown before the foundation of the world, but has appeared in these last times for the sake of you
NASB.21: who through Him are believers in God, who raised Him from the dead and gave Him glory, so that your faith and hope are in God.
NASB.22: Since you have in obedience to the truth purified your souls for a sincere love of the brethren, fervently love one another from the heart,
NASB.23: for you have been born again not of seed which is perishable but imperishable, [that is], through the living and enduring word of God.
NASB.24: For, 'ALL FLESH IS LIKE GRASS, AND ALL ITS GLORY LIKE THE FLOWER OF GRASS. THE GRASS WITHERS, AND THE FLOWER FALLS OFF,
NASB.25: BUT THE WORD OF THE LORD ENDURES FOREVER.' And this is the word which was preached to you.

NLT.1: This letter is from Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ. I am writing to God’s chosen people who are living as foreigners in the provinces of Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
NLT.2: God the Father knew you and chose you long ago, and his Spirit has made you holy. As a result, you have obeyed him and have been cleansed by the blood of Jesus Christ. May God give you more and more grace and peace.
NLT.3: All praise to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. It is by his great mercy that we have been born again, because God raised Jesus Christ from the dead. Now we live with great expectation,
NLT.4: and we have a priceless inheritance — an inheritance that is kept in heaven for you, pure and undefiled, beyond the reach of change and decay.
NLT.5: And through your faith, God is protecting you by his power until you receive this salvation, which is ready to be revealed on the last day for all to see.
NLT.6: So be truly glad. There is wonderful joy ahead, even though you must endure many trials for a little while.
NLT.7: These trials will show that your faith is genuine. It is being tested as fire tests and purifies gold — though your faith is far more precious than mere gold. So when your faith remains strong through many trials, it will bring you much praise and glory and honor on the day when Jesus Christ is revealed to the whole world.
NLT.8: You love him even though you have never seen him. Though you do not see him now, you trust him; and you rejoice with a glorious, inexpressible joy.
NLT.9: The reward for trusting him will be the salvation of your souls.
NLT.10: This salvation was something even the prophets wanted to know more about when they prophesied about this gracious salvation prepared for you.
NLT.11: They wondered what time or situation the Spirit of Christ within them was talking about when he told them in advance about Christ’s suffering and his great glory afterward.
NLT.12: They were told that their messages were not for themselves, but for you. And now this Good News has been announced to you by those who preached in the power of the Holy Spirit sent from heaven. It is all so wonderful that even the angels are eagerly watching these things happen.
NLT.13: So prepare your minds for action and exercise self-control. Put all your hope in the gracious salvation that will come to you when Jesus Christ is revealed to the world.
NLT.14: So you must live as God’s obedient children. Don’t slip back into your old ways of living to satisfy your own desires. You didn’t know any better then.
NLT.15: But now you must be holy in everything you do, just as God who chose you is holy.
NLT.16: For the Scriptures say, “You must be holy because I am holy.”
NLT.17: And remember that the heavenly Father to whom you pray has no favorites. He will judge or reward you according to what you do. So you must live in reverent fear of him during your time here as “temporary residents.”
NLT.18: For you know that God paid a ransom to save you from the empty life you inherited from your ancestors. And it was not paid with mere gold or silver, which lose their value.
NLT.19: It was the precious blood of Christ, the sinless, spotless Lamb of God.
NLT.20: God chose him as your ransom long before the world began, but now in these last days he has been revealed for your sake.
NLT.21: Through Christ you have come to trust in God. And you have placed your faith and hope in God because he raised Christ from the dead and gave him great glory.
NLT.22: You were cleansed from your sins when you obeyed the truth, so now you must show sincere love to each other as brothers and sisters. Love each other deeply with all your heart.
NLT.23: For you have been born again, but not to a life that will quickly end. Your new life will last forever because it comes from the eternal, living word of God.
NLT.24:
As the Scriptures say, “People are like grass;
their beauty is like a flower in the field.
The grass withers and the flower fades.
NLT.25:
But the word of the Lord remains forever.”
And that word is the Good News that was preached to you.

WEB.1: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the chosen ones who are living as foreigners in the Dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,
WEB.2: according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, in sanctification of the Spirit, that you may obey Jesus Christ and be sprinkled with his blood: Grace to you and peace be multiplied.
WEB.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who according to his great mercy caused us to be born again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
WEB.4: to an incorruptible and undefiled inheritance that doesn’t fade away, reserved in Heaven for you,
WEB.5: who by the power of God are guarded through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.
WEB.6: Wherein you greatly rejoice, though now for a little while, if need be, you have been put to grief in various trials,
WEB.7: that the proof of your faith, which is more precious than gold that perishes even though it is tested by fire, may be found to result in praise, glory, and honor at the revelation of Jesus Christ —
WEB.8: whom not having known you love; in whom, though now you don’t see him, yet believing, you rejoice greatly with joy unspeakable and full of glory —
WEB.9: receiving the result of your faith, the salvation of your souls.
WEB.10: Concerning this salvation, the prophets sought and searched diligently, who prophesied of the grace that would come to you,
WEB.11: searching for who or what kind of time the Spirit of Christ, which was in them, pointed to, when he predicted the sufferings of Christ, and the glories that would follow them.
WEB.12: To them it was revealed, that not to themselves, but to you, they ministered these things, which now have been announced to you through those who preached the Good News to you by the Holy Spirit sent out from heaven; which things angels desire to look into.
WEB.13: Therefore prepare your minds for action,  be sober, and set your hope fully on the grace that will be brought to you at the revelation of Jesus Christ —
WEB.14: as children of obedience, not conforming yourselves according to your former lusts as in your ignorance,
WEB.15: but just as he who called you is holy, you yourselves also be holy in all of your behavior;
WEB.16: because it is written, “You shall be holy; for I am holy.”
WEB.17: If you call on him as Father, who without respect of persons judges according to each man’s work, pass the time of your living as foreigners here in reverent fear:
WEB.18: knowing that you were redeemed, not with corruptible things, with silver or gold, from the useless way of life handed down from your fathers,
WEB.19: but with precious blood, as of a lamb without blemish or spot, the blood of Christ;
WEB.20: who was foreknown indeed before the foundation of the world, but was revealed in this last age for your sake,
WEB.21: who through him are believers in God, who raised him from the dead, and gave him glory; so that your faith and hope might be in God.
WEB.22: Seeing you have purified your souls in your obedience to the truth through the Spirit in sincere brotherly affection, love one another from the heart fervently:
WEB.23: having been born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, through the word of God, which lives and remains forever.
WEB.24: For, “All flesh is like grass, and all of man’s glory like the flower in the grass. The grass withers, and its flower falls;
WEB.25: but the Lord’s word endures forever.”   This is the word of Good News which was preached to you.

CSB17.1: Peter, <sup>ⓖ</sup> an apostle of Jesus Christ: To those chosen, living as exiles <sup>ⓗ</sup> dispersed <sup>ⓘ</sup> abroad <sup>[1]</sup> in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia, <sup>ⓙ</sup> chosen <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.2: according to the foreknowledge <sup>ⓛ</sup> of God the Father, through the sanctifying work of the Spirit, <sup>ⓜ</sup> to be obedient <sup>ⓝ</sup> and to be sprinkled with the blood <sup>ⓐ</sup> of Jesus Christ. May grace and peace be multiplied to you.
CSB17.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. <sup>ⓑ</sup> Because of his great mercy <sup>ⓒ</sup> he has given us new birth <sup>ⓓ</sup> into a living hope <sup>ⓔ</sup> through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.4: and into an inheritance that is imperishable, <sup>ⓖ</sup> undefiled, and unfading, kept in heaven <sup>ⓗ</sup> for you.
CSB17.5: You are being guarded by God’s power <sup>ⓘ</sup> through faith for a salvation that is ready to be revealed in the last time. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.6: You rejoice in this, <sup>[2]</sup> even though now for a short time, if necessary, you suffer grief in various trials <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.7: so that the proven character of your faith <sup>ⓛ</sup>  — more valuable than gold which, though perishable, is refined by fire <sup>ⓜ</sup>  — may result <sup>ⓝ</sup> in praise, glory, and honor <sup>ⓐ</sup> at the revelation of Jesus Christ. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.8: Though you have not seen <sup>ⓒ</sup> him, you love him; <sup>ⓓ</sup> though not seeing him now, you believe in him, and you rejoice <sup>ⓔ</sup> with inexpressible and glorious joy,
CSB17.9: because you are receiving the goal of your faith, the salvation of your souls.
CSB17.10: Concerning this salvation, the prophets, <sup>ⓕ</sup> who prophesied about the grace that would come to you, searched and carefully investigated.
CSB17.11: They inquired into what time or what circumstances the Spirit of Christ <sup>ⓖ</sup> within them was indicating when he testified in advance <sup>ⓗ</sup> to the sufferings of Christ <sup>ⓘ</sup> and the glories <sup>ⓙ</sup> that would follow. <sup>[3]</sup>
CSB17.12: It was revealed to them that they were not serving themselves but you. These things have now been announced to you through those who preached the gospel to you <sup>ⓚ</sup> by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven <sup>ⓛ</sup>  — angels long to catch a glimpse of these things.
CSB17.13: Therefore, with your minds ready for action, <sup>ⓜ</sup> be sober-minded <sup>ⓝ</sup> and set your hope <sup>ⓐ</sup> completely on the grace to be brought to you at the revelation <sup>ⓑ</sup> of Jesus Christ.
CSB17.14: As obedient children, <sup>ⓒ</sup> do not be conformed to the desires of your former ignorance.
CSB17.15: But as the one who called you <sup>ⓓ</sup> is holy, <sup>ⓔ</sup> you also are to be holy in all your conduct;
CSB17.16: for it is written, Be holy, because I am holy. [4] ⓕ
CSB17.17: If you appeal to the Father who judges impartially <sup>ⓖ</sup> according to each one’s work, <sup>ⓗ</sup> you are to conduct yourselves in reverence <sup>ⓘ</sup> during your time living as strangers.
CSB17.18: For you know that you were redeemed from your empty way of life <sup>ⓙ</sup> inherited from your fathers, not with perishable things like silver or gold, <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.19: but with the precious blood of Christ, <sup>ⓛ</sup> like that of an unblemished and spotless lamb. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.20: He was foreknown <sup>ⓝ</sup> before the foundation of the world but was revealed in these last times <sup>ⓐ</sup> for you.
CSB17.21: Through him you believe in God, <sup>ⓑ</sup> who raised him from the dead <sup>ⓒ</sup> and gave him glory, <sup>ⓓ</sup> so that your faith and hope are in God.
CSB17.22: Since you have purified yourselves by your obedience to the truth, <sup>[5]</sup> so that you show sincere brotherly love for each other, <sup>ⓔ</sup> from a pure <sup>[6]</sup> heart love one another constantly, <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.23: because you have been born again <sup>ⓖ</sup>  — not of perishable seed <sup>ⓗ</sup> but of imperishable — through the living and enduring word of God. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.24:
For All flesh is like grass,
and all its glory ⓙ like a flower of the grass.
The grass withers, and the flower falls,
CSB17.25:
but the word of the Lord endures forever. [8] ⓚ
And this word is the gospel that was proclaimed to you.

AMP.1:
Peter, an apostle (special messenger, personally chosen representative) of Jesus Christ,
To those [elect—both Jewish and Gentile believers] who live as exiles, scattered throughout Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia [Minor], and Bithynia, who are chosen
AMP.2: according to the foreknowledge of God the Father by the sanctifying work of the Spirit to be obedient to Jesus Christ and to be sprinkled with His blood: May grace and peace [that special sense of spiritual well-being] be yours in increasing abundance [as you walk closely with God].
AMP.3: Blessed [gratefully praised and adored] be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who according to His abundant and boundless mercy has caused us to be born again [that is, to be reborn from above—spiritually transformed, renewed, and set apart for His purpose] to an ever-living hope and confident assurance through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
AMP.4: [born anew] into an inheritance which is imperishable [beyond the reach of change] and undefiled and unfading, reserved in heaven for you,
AMP.5: who are being protected and shielded by the power of God through your faith for salvation that is ready to be revealed [for you] in the last time.
AMP.6: In this you rejoice greatly, even though now for a little while, if necessary, you have been distressed by various trials,
AMP.7: so that the genuineness of your faith, which is much more precious than gold which is perishable, even though tested and purified by fire, may be found to result in [your] praise and glory and honor at the revelation of Jesus Christ.
AMP.8: Though you have not seen Him, you love Him; and though you do not even see Him now, you believe and trust in Him and you greatly rejoice and delight with inexpressible and glorious joy,
AMP.9: receiving as the result [the outcome, the consummation] of your faith, the salvation of your souls.
AMP.10: Regarding this salvation, the prophets who prophesied about the grace [of God] that was intended for you, searched carefully and inquired [about this future way of salvation],
AMP.11: seeking to find out what person or what time the Spirit of Christ within them was indicating as He foretold the sufferings of Christ and the glories [destined] to follow.
AMP.12: It was revealed to them that their services [their prophecies regarding grace] were not [meant] for themselves and their time, but for you, in these things [the death, resurrection, and glorification of Jesus Christ] which have now been told to you by those who preached the gospel to you by the [power of the] Holy Spirit [who was] sent from heaven. Into these things even the angels long to look.
AMP.13: So prepare your minds for action, be completely sober [in spirit—steadfast, self-disciplined, spiritually and morally alert], fix your hope completely on the grace [of God] that is coming to you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
AMP.14: [Live] as obedient children [of God]; do not be conformed to the evil desires which governed you in your ignorance [before you knew the requirements and transforming power of the good news regarding salvation].
AMP.15: But like the Holy One who called you, be holy yourselves in all your conduct [be set apart from the world by your godly character and moral courage];
AMP.16: because it is written, "<e>You shall be holy</e> (set apart), <e>for I am holy</e>."
AMP.17: If you address as Father, the One who impartially judges according to each one's work, conduct yourselves in [reverent] fear [of Him] and with profound respect for Him throughout the time of your stay on earth.
AMP.18: For you know that you were not redeemed from your useless [spiritually unproductive] way of life inherited [by tradition] from your forefathers with perishable things like silver and gold,
AMP.19: but [you were actually purchased] with precious blood, like that of a [sacrificial] lamb unblemished and spotless, the priceless blood of Christ.
AMP.20: For He was foreordained (foreknown) before the foundation of the world, but has appeared [publicly] in these last times for your sake
AMP.21: and through Him you believe [confidently] in God [the heavenly Father], who raised Him from the dead and gave Him glory, so that your faith and hope are [centered and rest] in God.
AMP.22: Since by your obedience to the truth you have purified yourselves for a sincere love of the believers, [see that you] love one another from the heart [always unselfishly seeking the best for one another],
AMP.23: for you have been born again [that is, reborn from above—spiritually transformed, renewed, and set apart for His purpose] not of seed which is perishable but [from that which is] imperishable and immortal, that is, through the living and everlasting word of God.
AMP.24:
For,
"All flesh is like grass,
And all its glory like the flower of grass.
The grass withers
And the flower falls off,
AMP.25:
But the word of the Lord endures forever."
And this is the word [the good news of salvation] which was preached to you.

MSG.1: I, Peter, am an apostle on assignment by Jesus, the Messiah, writing to exiles scattered to the four winds. Not one is missing, not one forgotten.
MSG.2: God the Father has his eye on each of you, and has determined by the work of the Spirit to keep you obedient through the sacrifice of Jesus. May everything good from God be yours!
MSG.3: What a God we have! And how fortunate we are to have him, this Father of our Master Jesus! Because Jesus was raised from the dead, we've been given a brand-new life and have everything to live for,
MSG.4: including a future in heaven—and the future starts now!
MSG.5: God is keeping careful watch over us and the future. The Day is coming when you'll have it all—life healed and whole.
MSG.6: I know how great this makes you feel, even though you have to put up with every kind of aggravation in the meantime.
MSG.7: Pure gold put in the fire comes out of it proved pure; genuine faith put through this suffering comes out proved genuine. When Jesus wraps this all up, it's your faith, not your gold, that God will have on display as evidence of his victory.
MSG.8: You never saw him, yet you love him. You still don't see him, yet you trust him—with laughter and singing.
MSG.9: Because you kept on believing, you'll get what you're looking forward to: total salvation.
MSG.10: The prophets who told us this was coming asked a lot of questions about this gift of life God was preparing.
MSG.11: The Messiah's Spirit let them in on some of it—that the Messiah would experience suffering, followed by glory. They clamored to know who and when.
MSG.12: All they were told was that they were serving you, you who by orders from heaven have now heard for yourselves—through the Holy Spirit—the Message of those prophecies fulfilled. Do you realize how fortunate you are? Angels would have given anything to be in on this!
MSG.13: So roll up your sleeves, put your mind in gear, be totally ready to receive the gift that's coming when Jesus arrives.
MSG.14: Don't lazily slip back into those old grooves of evil, doing just what you feel like doing. You didn't know any better then; you do now.
MSG.15: As obedient children, let yourselves be pulled into a way of life shaped by God's life, a life energetic and blazing with holiness.
MSG.16: God said, "I am holy; you be holy."
MSG.17: You call out to God for help and he helps—he's a good Father that way. But don't forget, he's also a responsible Father, and won't let you get by with sloppy living. Your life is a journey you must travel with a deep consciousness of God.
MSG.18: It cost God plenty to get you out of that dead-end, empty-headed life you grew up in.
MSG.19: He paid with Christ's sacred blood, you know. He died like an unblemished, sacrificial lamb.
MSG.20: And this was no afterthought. Even though it has only lately—at the end of the ages—become public knowledge, God always knew he was going to do this for you.
MSG.21: It's because of this sacrificed Messiah, whom God then raised from the dead and glorified, that you trust God, that you know you have a future in God.
MSG.22: Now that you've cleaned up your lives by following the truth, love one another as if your lives depended on it.
MSG.23: Your new life is not like your old life. Your old birth came from mortal sperm; your new birth comes from God's living Word. Just think: a life conceived by God himself!
MSG.24: That's why the prophet said, The old life is a grass life, its beauty as short-lived as wildflowers; Grass dries up, flowers droop,
MSG.25: God's Word goes on and on forever. This is the Word that conceived the new life in you.

NIV.1:
Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ,
To God’s elect, strangers in the world, scattered throughout Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia and Bithynia,
NIV.2:
who have been chosen according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, through the sanctifying work of the Spirit, for obedience to Jesus Christ and sprinkling by his blood:
Grace and peace be yours in abundance.
NIV.3:
Praise to God for a Living Hope
Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! In his great mercy he has given us new birth into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
NIV.4: and into an inheritance that can never perish, spoil or fade—kept in heaven for you,
NIV.5: who through faith are shielded by God’s power until the coming of the salvation that is ready to be revealed in the last time.
NIV.6: In this you greatly rejoice, though now for a little while you may have had to suffer grief in all kinds of trials.
NIV.7: These have come so that your faith—of greater worth than gold, which perishes even though refined by fire—may be proved genuine and may result in praise, glory and honor when Jesus Christ is revealed.
NIV.8: Though you have not seen him, you love him; and even though you do not see him now, you believe in him and are filled with an inexpressible and glorious joy,
NIV.9: for you are receiving the goal of your faith, the salvation of your souls.
NIV.10: Concerning this salvation, the prophets, who spoke of the grace that was to come to you, searched intently and with the greatest care,
NIV.11: trying to find out the time and circumstances to which the Spirit of Christ in them was pointing when he predicted the sufferings of Christ and the glories that would follow.
NIV.12: It was revealed to them that they were not serving themselves but you, when they spoke of the things that have now been told you by those who have preached the gospel to you by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven. Even angels long to look into these things.
NIV.13:
Be Holy
Therefore, prepare your minds for action; be self-controlled; set your hope fully on the grace to be given you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
NIV.14: As obedient children, do not conform to the evil desires you had when you lived in ignorance.
NIV.15: But just as he who called you is holy, so be holy in all you do;
NIV.16: for it is written: “Be holy, because I am holy.”
NIV.17: Since you call on a Father who judges each man’s work impartially, live your lives as strangers here in reverent fear.
NIV.18: For you know that it was not with perishable things such as silver or gold that you were redeemed from the empty way of life handed down to you from your forefathers,
NIV.19: but with the precious blood of Christ, a lamb without blemish or defect.
NIV.20: He was chosen before the creation of the world, but was revealed in these last times for your sake.
NIV.21: Through him you believe in God, who raised him from the dead and glorified him, and so your faith and hope are in God.
NIV.22: Now that you have purified yourselves by obeying the truth so that you have sincere love for your brothers, love one another deeply, from the heart.
NIV.23: For you have been born again, not of perishable seed, but of imperishable, through the living and enduring word of God.
NIV.24:
For,
“All men are like grass,
and all their glory is like the flowers of the field;
the grass withers and the flowers fall,
NIV.25:
but the word of the Lord stands forever.”
And this is the word that was preached to you.

NRSVCE.1: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To the exiles of the Dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,
NRSVCE.2: who have been chosen and destined by God the Father and sanctified by the Spirit to be obedient to Jesus Christ and to be sprinkled with his blood: May grace and peace be yours in abundance.
NRSVCE.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! By his great mercy he has given us a new birth into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
NRSVCE.4: and into an inheritance that is imperishable, undefiled, and unfading, kept in heaven for you,
NRSVCE.5: who are being protected by the power of God through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.
NRSVCE.6: In this you rejoice, even if now for a little while you have had to suffer various trials,
NRSVCE.7: so that the genuineness of your faith — being more precious than gold that, though perishable, is tested by fire — may be found to result in praise and glory and honor when Jesus Christ is revealed.
NRSVCE.8: Although you have not seen him, you love him; and even though you do not see him now, you believe in him and rejoice with an indescribable and glorious joy,
NRSVCE.9: for you are receiving the outcome of your faith, the salvation of your souls.
NRSVCE.10: Concerning this salvation, the prophets who prophesied of the grace that was to be yours made careful search and inquiry,
NRSVCE.11: inquiring about the person or time that the Spirit of Christ within them indicated when it testified in advance to the sufferings destined for Christ and the subsequent glory.
NRSVCE.12: It was revealed to them that they were serving not themselves but you, in regard to the things that have now been announced to you through those who brought you good news by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven — things into which angels long to look!
NRSVCE.13: Therefore prepare your minds for action; discipline yourselves; set all your hope on the grace that Jesus Christ will bring you when he is revealed.
NRSVCE.14: Like obedient children, do not be conformed to the desires that you formerly had in ignorance.
NRSVCE.15: Instead, as he who called you is holy, be holy yourselves in all your conduct;
NRSVCE.16: for it is written, “You shall be holy, for I am holy.”
NRSVCE.17: If you invoke as Father the one who judges all people impartially according to their deeds, live in reverent fear during the time of your exile.
NRSVCE.18: You know that you were ransomed from the futile ways inherited from your ancestors, not with perishable things like silver or gold,
NRSVCE.19: but with the precious blood of Christ, like that of a lamb without defect or blemish.
NRSVCE.20: He was destined before the foundation of the world, but was revealed at the end of the ages for your sake.
NRSVCE.21: Through him you have come to trust in God, who raised him from the dead and gave him glory, so that your faith and hope are set on God.
NRSVCE.22: Now that you have purified your souls by your obedience to the truth so that you have genuine mutual love, love one another deeply from the heart.
NRSVCE.23: You have been born anew, not of perishable but of imperishable seed, through the living and enduring word of God.
NRSVCE.24:
For “All flesh is like grass
and all its glory like the flower of grass.
The grass withers,
and the flower falls,
NRSVCE.25:
but the word of the Lord endures forever.”
That word is the good news that was announced to you.

NET.1: From Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to those temporarily residing abroad (in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, the province of Asia, and Bithynia) who are chosen
NET.2: according to the foreknowledge of God the Father by being set apart by the Spirit for obedience and for sprinkling with Jesus Christ’s blood. May grace and peace be yours in full measure!
NET.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! By his great mercy he gave us new birth into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
NET.4: that is, into an inheritance imperishable, undefiled, and unfading. It is reserved in heaven for you,
NET.5: who by God’s power are protected through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.
NET.6: This brings you great joy, although you may have to suffer for a short time in various trials.
NET.7: Such trials show the proven character of your faith, which is much more valuable than gold — gold that is tested by fire, even though it is passing away — and will bring praise and glory and honor when Jesus Christ is revealed.
NET.8: You have not seen him, but you love him. You do not see him now but you believe in him, and so you rejoice with an indescribable and glorious joy,
NET.9: because you are attaining the goal of your faith — the salvation of your souls.
NET.10: Concerning this salvation, the prophets who predicted the grace that would come to you searched and investigated carefully.
NET.11: They probed into what person or time the Spirit of Christ within them was indicating when he testified beforehand about the sufferings appointed for Christ and his subsequent glory.
NET.12: They were shown that they were serving not themselves but you, in regard to the things now announced to you through those who proclaimed the gospel to you by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven — things angels long to catch a glimpse of.
NET.13: Therefore, get your minds ready for action by being fully sober, and set your hope completely on the grace that will be brought to you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
NET.14: Like obedient children, do not comply with the evil urges you used to follow in your ignorance,
NET.15: but, like the Holy One who called you, become holy yourselves in all of your conduct,
NET.16: for it is written, “You shall be holy, because I am holy.”
NET.17: And if you address as Father the one who impartially judges according to each one’s work, live out the time of your temporary residence here in reverence.
NET.18: You know that from your empty way of life inherited from your ancestors you were ransomed — not by perishable things like silver or gold,
NET.19: but by precious blood like that of an unblemished and spotless lamb, namely Christ.
NET.20: He was foreknown before the foundation of the world but was manifested in these last times for your sake.
NET.21: Through him you now trust in God, who raised him from the dead and gave him glory, so that your faith and hope are in God.
NET.22: You have purified your souls by obeying the truth in order to show sincere mutual love. So love one another earnestly from a pure heart.
NET.23: You have been born anew, not from perishable but from imperishable seed, through the living and enduring word of God.
NET.24: For all flesh is like grass and all its glory like the flower of the grass; the grass withers and the flower falls off,
NET.25: but the word of the Lord endures forever. And this is the word that was proclaimed to you.
